title: Portland Pivot Kick Study: Exam Maneuver for Meniscus Tears Prediction
author: Samuel Moulton
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7d2hi8e

steps.instruction: The study timeline is as follows:

Initial visit : The patient will be evaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. Arthroscopic partial meniscectomy will be offered as a surgical option to the patient. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.
6 week post operative follow up: The patient will be reevaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.
6 month post operative follow up: The patient will be reevaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.
1 year post operative follow up: The patient will be reevaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.
The patient will perform the following home exercises during the postoperative period:

Preoperative visit: The patient will be evaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. Arthroscopic partial meniscectomy will be offered as a surgical option to the patient. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.
